title: DFIRCLI Container frontmatter
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter of a substrate file under data/substrates/<name>.md. Each file defines one substrate (binary-structured-file / plaintext-log / etc) that hosts artifacts.

name.description: Container-class identifier. Must match the filename without .md and match every artifact's substrate field that lives here.

kind.description: Substrate type — governs how the container is acquired and parsed.

substrate-class.description: Taxonomic substrate-class. Slash-path form (Parent/Child) groups multiple sibling classes under one parent for substrate-view mother synthesis. Drives coloring and hull clustering. Old flat values retained as deprecated-aliases for one migration cycle.

format.description: Format descriptors: magic, endianness, version, authoritative-spec, etc.

persistence.description: Live-system location, rotation, retention, acquisition methods.

forensic-relevance.description: Structured or free-form notes on forensic relevance (either an array of keyed objects or a single object).

known-artifacts.description: Roster of authored + known-unwritten artifacts that belong to this container. Accepts either a flat list of names or an {authored, unwritten} split. Build validates authored names against actual files; unwritten names produce graph ghost nodes.

instance-implications.description: Per substrate-instance concept implications — concepts the substrate imposes on every artifact in that instance, independent of the artifact's own references-data.

provenance.description: Source attribution at the substrate level. Each item is either a source-registry ID (string) or an object {source, section?, note?}. Every ID must resolve to an entry in schema/sources.yaml. Substrate-level sources are format specs, parser catalogs, acquisition-method references, and other documentation that informs the substrate (container format) itself rather than specific artifacts within.
